purpose: Compact current-testnet STP drill evidence after SatoshiNet rollback: open, expand, unlock, lock, BRC20 splicing-out, retained old Core Node commitment, and client punish package.
notes: This evidence uses current v3/code-backed endpoints and actual indexer/RPC responses, not stale Swagger output.
This compact run proves current-testnet punish capability. The fuller multi-asset video storyline can still use the earlier Runes+BRC20 evidence package where both assets were completed in one run.
Runes recovery was not forced in this compact run because the available candidate was tied to an old drained anchor state. The Agent stopped instead of inventing unsafe asset evidence.

operations.retained_deanchor_status: constructed and locally accepted during retention; no current public txid is listed because the old-state deAnchor is not visible on the current L2 indexer

operations.note: The old retained deAnchor was not rebroadcast after state advance; missing-inputs is expected for this old-state drill.

operations.result: old Core Node commitment and all punish transactions confirmed in the same BTC testnet4 block

post_punish_observation.interpretation: The old punished L1 state and the current channel record are separate evidence lines. Agent-facing reports must not merge them into one state transition.

agent_rules_validated: Do not rely on service success strings alone; query every returned L1 txid after package broadcast.
If peer/network result is unknown before final acknowledgement, local state must not advance without peer evidence.
If an asset recovery candidate cannot be safely tied to current channel evidence, stop and report the ambiguity instead of forcing an expand.
Use current v3/code-backed endpoints and actual runtime responses for validation.
